# @platform/infra-cloudflare

Cloudflare infrastructure definitions: CDN, R2 object storage, Pages deployment, and DNS/WAF configuration.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/infra-cloudflare
Version: 0.1.0
Component: infra-cloudflare
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: delivery
Bounded context: cloudflare-infra
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: tooling
Class: active.tooling
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: false
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Define Terraform configurations for Cloudflare CDN, WAF rules, and DNS records
- Configure Cloudflare R2 buckets as a free-tier alternative to S3 for static assets and media
- Manage Cloudflare Pages deployment configuration for the front-end application shell
- Define Cache rules, Page rules, and rate-limiting policies

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not own AWS infrastructure definitions (infra-aws)
- Does not define Terraform workflow tooling or module patterns (tooling-terraform)
- Does not manage Cloudflare Workers or edge compute — kept out of scope initially

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Applied via tooling-terraform scripts as part of the front-end delivery pipeline in tooling-ci
- Complements infra-aws: Cloudflare handles CDN/edge layer, AWS handles compute and data

## Operational notes

- Cloudflare free tier covers CDN, R2 (10GB storage, 1M operations/month), and Pages — monitor usage before upgrading
- R2 egress is free; use for static assets and media to avoid AWS data transfer costs
- WAF rules must be reviewed quarterly for emerging threat patterns
